steps.title: Identify the key evidence
steps.content: When the research team modified the timing of downbeats and offbeats, the modified songs were more than seven times more likely to be characterized as having swing than the originals.

steps.title: Apply the evidence to the choices
steps.content: The evidence shows that modifying the timing of downbeats and offbeats causes a dramatic increase in swing perception—so timing must matter for creating swing. Choice D states this directly: the timing of downbeats and offbeats may play a crucial role in giving a song swing. The other choices isolate one timing element only, contradict the successful manipulation shown, or address a variable never tested. That matches choice D — D is correct.
